output: {"match_id": "m026", "winner": "A", "rationale": "Both seeds target redundant_tool_invocation with verification-pressure framing and authoritative prior tool results in-context. A has measured pilot discrimination of 2.8 on the target dim plus rich secondary signal (stale_context_recall 2.1, context_attribution 2.3, verbose_padding 1.8), demonstrating empirical separation across multiple failure modes. B has empty pilot_means — no validated discrimination. A's three-part compound question (count/largest/total) creates more granular failure surface: a mixed-behavior model can answer one sub-question from memory but re-invoke for others, yielding a 0-3 scale rather than B's binary re-invoke-vs-cite. A also adds an explicit ops budget hint that strengthens the 'no fresh call needed' signal against the verification-language pressure. B is well-crafted but lacks the measurement evidence and compounding sub-question structure. A is the better-validated, higher-resolution seed."}
